headline: Altruist's AI platform launch causes $150B wealth management market sell-off
summary: When Altruist announced its Hazel AI platform — a fully autonomous financial planning assistant capable of replacing certain advisor functions — publicly traded wealth management companies collectively lost $150 billion in market capitalization over three trading days. The market reaction signals that institutional investors view AI-native RIA platforms as a genuine structural threat to traditional advisory models. Hazel can generate comprehensive financial plans, rebalance portfolios, and handle client onboarding autonomously at a fraction of the cost of human advisors. The event is being called the 'Napster moment' for wealth management.

key_stat.label: Market cap lost on Altruist Hazel launch
